title: crataegus monogyna laciniata
intro: Crataegus monogyna laciniata, also known as the cut-leaf hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to improve cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation. The leaves and berries of the cut-leaf hawthorn contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to provide various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and improving cardiac function. Cut-leaf hawthorn can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal remedies to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
